fault_hypothesis: The expiry check on line 68 uses strict greater-than (`now > effective`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= effective`), so when `now == effective` the entry is incorrectly returned as valid instead of expired.

constraints.intent: At the boundary check, the condition should treat now == effective as expired (now >= effective should cause return None), so the branch must use >= not >.
After the expiry branch is not taken (else branch), now must be strictly less than effective; equality should have triggered the then-branch.
After computing effective, verify the semantic: if now >= effective the function must return None, not value.
The function should only return a non-None value when now is strictly less than effective (not equal).
